Future
Developer.

Jamari graduated with a builder's mind. Here's where that curiosity is pointed next — and why we're betting on him.

See the roadmapThe photos
Scroll
"The people who are crazy enough to think they can change the world are the ones who do."
— and Jamari's the kind of crazy that codes
Why programming

A graduate who'd rather build the tool than just use it

🧩

Problem solver

Code is just structured problem-solving. Jamari's been doing that his whole life — now he's got the language for it.

⚡

Fast learner

The best developers aren't the ones who know everything — they're the ones who learn fastest. That's the move.

🌍

Wants to build for people

Apps, sites, games, tools — software is how one person's idea reaches millions. He gets that.

The Roadmap

From here to shipping code

2026 · NOW

🎓 High school graduate

Monarch High, Class of 2026. Diploma in hand, momentum at his back.

STEP 01

Learn the fundamentals

HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and the logic of programming. Build small, build often, break things and fix them.

STEP 02

Build a portfolio

Real projects beat any résumé. A few sites and apps he can point to and say "I made that."

STEP 03

Go deeper

Pick a lane — web, mobile, games, AI — and get seriously good at it. Contribute, collaborate, ship.

THE FUTURE

🚀 Software developer

Internships, a degree or a bootcamp, a first dev job — and eventually, products with his name on them.

The toolkit ahead

Languages & tech on the horizon

HTMLCSSJavaScriptPython ReactNode.jsGit & GitHubSQL APIsThree.jsAI / ML…and whatever's next
